perturbation.original_text: (b) Receiving Party shall take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, to keep confidential the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Receiving Party shall take minimal security precautions to keep confidential the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change reduces the required level of security precautions. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity because earlier the agreement stated 'reasonable security precautions', now it states 'minimal security precautions', creating uncertainty about the actual security obligations. This shift from 'reasonable' to 'minimal' creates direct ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions ------------ (a) Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties for five (5) years following the date of its disclosure by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, except to Receiving Party's consultants as provided below. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order or equivalent. (b) Receiving Party shall take minimal security precautions to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Material only to Receiving Party's employees or consultants on a need-to-know basis. Receiving Party will have executed or shall execute appropriate written agreements with its employees and consultants sufficient to enable it to comply with all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the requirement for security precautions from 'reasonable' to 'minimal'. The contradicted text repeats the 'minimal' security precaution requirement. While the texts are similar, the original text stated 'reasonable security precautions', while the change text modifies it to 'minimal security precautions', causing a contradiction. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the actual security obligations because 'reasonable' and 'minimal' imply different standards of care, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguity as defined by the perturbation explanation and the inconsistencies definition.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages shall be the only remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the remedies available to the Disclosing Party, stating monetary damages are the exclusive remedy. This in-text contradiction negates the original allowance for injunctive or equitable relief, creating uncertainty about the available legal recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights and Remedies ------------------- (a) Receiving Party shall notify Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials, or any other breach of this Agreement by <PAGE> Receiving Party, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information and/or Confidential Materials and prevent its further unauthorized use. (b) Receiving Party shall return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information or Confidential Materials at Disclosing Party's request, or at Disclosing Party's option, certify destruction of the same. (c) Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages shall be the only remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information. (d) Disclosing Party may visit Receiving Party's premises, with reasonable prior notice and during normal business hours, to review Receiving Party's compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (c) to state that "monetary damages shall be the only remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information." The contradicted text includes the same modified section (c) but also includes sections (a), (b), and (d). The explanation states that the change creates an in-text contradiction because it negates the original allowance for injunctive or equitable relief. Although the changed text and contradicted text contain the same modified section, the changed section creates a contradiction to the original allowance for injunctive relief, and therefore, the inclusion of the changed section in the contradicted text creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (f) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (f) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It can be modified by either a written agreement or verbal agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and agreed upon by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows for verbal modifications to the agreement, contradicting the initial requirement for written modifications only. This in-text contradiction raises uncertainty in enforcement because it becomes unclear which modifications are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It can be modified by either a written agreement or verbal agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and agreed upon by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, its agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by <PAGE> an authorized officer of Disclosing Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (f) to allow for both written and verbal agreements to modify the contract, directly contradicting the original requirement for only written agreements. This inconsistency with the contradicted text creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity regarding which form of modification is valid, thus impacting enforceability. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that all Feedback is and shall be entirely voluntary and shall not, absent separate agreement, create any confidentiality obligation for the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that all Feedback is mandatory and shall create a confidentiality obligation for the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change mandates feedback and creates a confidentiality obligation for the Receiving Party regarding that feedback. This in-text contradiction because the initial agreement says that Feedback shall be entirely voluntary, creates ambiguity and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Suggestions and Feedback ------------------------ Either party may from time to time provide suggestions, comments or other feedback to the other party with respect to Confidential Information provided originally by the other party (hereinafter "Feedback"). Both parties agree that all Feedback is mandatory and shall create a confidentiality obligation for the Receiving Party. However, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the source of any feedback without the providing party's consent. Feedback shall be clearly designated as such and, except as otherwise provided herein, each party shall be free to disclose and use such Feedback as it sees fit, entirely without obligation of any kind to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that feedback is mandatory and creates a confidentiality obligation, while the contradicted text initially states that feedback is mandatory and creates a confidentiality obligation, but then later provides that each party is free to disclose and use such Feedback as it sees fit, entirely without obligation of any kind to the other party. The first part creates an in-text contradiction with the last part because it is not clear how the feedback can be free to disclose and use if it is mandatory and there is a confidentiality obligation, adhering to the definition of inconsistencies causing in-text contradiction.
